      <title>Kingston Flemings Declares For 2026 NBA Draft</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285182/Kingston-Flemings-Declares-For-2026-NBA-Draft</link>
      <description><p>University of Houston guard Kingston Flemings has declared as an Early Entry Candidate for the 2026 NBA Draft. Flemings is declaring after his freshman season with the Cougars.</p>
<p>ESPN has Flemings ranked as the seventh-best prospect in their Top 100 for the 2026 draft. He's the fourth-ranked guard behind Darryn Peterson, Keaton Wagler and Darius Acuff Jr.</p>
<p>In his lone season with the Cougars, the 6-foot-3 guard averaged 16.1 points, 4.1 rebounds, 5.2 assists and 1.5 steals. Flemings shot 47.6% from the field, including 38.7% on three-pointers. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 19 Apr 2026 15:59:55 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285182/Kingston-Flemings-Declares-For-2026-NBA-Draft</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Flemings_Kingston_uh_251223.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Kristaps Porzingis To Take Some Time To Figure Out Career Direction</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285173/Kristaps-Porzingis-To-Take-Some-Time-To-Figure-Out-Career-Direction</link>
      <description><p>Kristaps Porzingis wasn't happy his team lost in the Play-In Tournament, but was happy that he had a productive finish for the Golden State Warriors.</p>
<p>"It honestly was a big step forward for me," Porzingis said. "To have a game like this, it means a lot. To pull it out of somewhere, and it was there. Definitely a big step in the right direction."</p>
<p>As for what's next, the veteran center said he plans to take his time before making any decisions. Porzingis will be an unrestricted free agent in July.</p>
<p>"That's a good question," Porzingis responded when asked if he wants to re-sign with Golden State. "I do love my time here, honestly. Not one bad thing I can say about the organization, the team, the teammates. I really love it here, I really do. As I said before, I haven't had a good year. If I had a good year, I think I'd have a more clear picture of what I want to do. Because I've had an up-and-down year like this, this is an offseason in my career that I think I'm just going to take a step back, look at the whole picture and then see what's the best direction for me."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 19 Apr 2026 09:19:20 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285173/Kristaps-Porzingis-To-Take-Some-Time-To-Figure-Out-Career-Direction</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Porzingis_Kristaps_gsw_260306.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Thunder Open Championship Defense With 35-Point Blowout Of Suns</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285186/Thunder-Open-Championship-Defense-With-35-Point-Blowout-Of-Suns</link>
      <description><p>The Oklahoma City Thunder launched their title defense with a 119-84 dismantling of the Phoenix Suns in Game 1 Sunday at Paycom Center, matching last postseason's opening blowout of the Memphis Grizzlies  by again winning by at least 35 points. The Thunder became just the third team in NBA history to open back-to-back postseasons with wins of that margin, joining the Los Angeles Lakers of 1986 and 1987.</p>
<p>Oklahoma City converted 19 Suns turnovers into 34 points, producing a 34-2 advantage in points off turnovers, the largest such differential recorded in a playoff game during the play-by-play era.</p>
<p>"Every time you're playing against them, you've got to value the ball, value possessions, got to get a shot up -- and we did not do that," Suns coach Jordan Ott said. "Their physicality, their athleticism, their size, their length [and] their personnel forces you to continuously make good decisions and quick decisions. That's part of it for sure that we'll have to clean up."</p>
<p>Jalen Williams paced Oklahoma City with 22 points, seven rebounds, and six assists. Reigning MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander contributed 25 points and seven assists despite shooting 5-of-18 and sitting out the fourth quarter.</p>
<p>"I'm sure those guys take the regular-season game serious, but they feel like this is the time of year," Devin Booker said. "Every time down, [we can't] turn it over. I think that fuels everything -- fuels them, fuels the crowd. Turns into a domino effect."</p>
<p>Phoenix faced a difficult path into Game 1, having secured their playoff spot just Friday night with a play-in victory over the Golden State Warriors before traveling Saturday for a 2:30 p.m. local tipoff against the top overall seed on Sunday.</p>
<p>The Thunder led by 15 after one quarter, 21 at halftime, and 31 heading into the fourth. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 19 Apr 2026 23:05:24 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285186/Thunder-Open-Championship-Defense-With-35-Point-Blowout-Of-Suns</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Gilgeousalexander_Shai_okc_250420.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Amari Allen Declares For 2026 NBA Draft</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285184/Amari-Allen-Declares-For-2026-NBA-Draft</link>
      <description><p>University of Alabama forward Amari Allen announced that he's declaring as an Early Entry Candidate for the 2026 NBA Draft. Allen is leaving the Crimson Tide after his freshman season.</p>
<p>The 6-foot-8 forward appeared in 32 games for Alabama as a freshman. Allen averaged 11.4 points, 6.9 rebounds, 3.1 assists and a steal per game. He shot 44.6% from the field and 73.8% on free throws.</p>
<p>ESPN ranks Allen as their 29th best prospect for the 2026 NBA Draft.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 19 Apr 2026 22:26:03 EDT</pubDate>

   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Celtics Rout 76ers 123-91 In Game 1, Jayson Tatum Posts 25-Point Double-Double</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285185/Celtics-Rout-76ers-123-91-In-Game-1-Jayson-Tatum-Posts-25-Point-Double-Double</link>
      <description><p>The Boston Celtics opened their first round series with a commanding 123-91 victory over the Philadelphia 76ers on Sunday.</p>
<p>Jayson Tatum led Boston with 25 points, 11 rebounds, and seven assists in 32 minutes as he continues his remarkable recovery from  a torn Achilles tendon less than a year ago. Jaylen Brown added 26 points as the Celtics led wire-to-wire and pushed their advantage to 35 points at its peak.</p>
<p>"That was Celtics basketball," Brown said. "Our offense can't win us this series. I mean, our offense is important, but we got to make sure we guard. And I think tonight we did a great job."</p>
<p>Philadelphia shot 4-for-23 from three-point range and surrendered a 22-3 deficit in points off turnovers. Tyrese Maxey finished with 21 points on 8-for-21 shooting.</p>
<p>"The weird part about it is we kind of got some good looks," Maxey said. "We just missed wide-open 3s. But the defense, we had a lot of defensive breakdowns, a lot of stuff that we went over, that can't happen."</p>
<p>Joel Embiid, recovering from an emergency appendectomy performed April 9, stayed in Philadelphia and did not travel with the team. The 76ers' primary objective is stretching the series long enough to give the star center a chance to return.</p>
<p>"A lot went wrong, that's for sure," 76ers coach Nick Nurse said. "I thought from the second quarter to about midway through the third, we finally got ourselves underneath us a little bit and played pretty good basketball.</p>
<p>"But the minutes around that was absolutely unacceptable."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 19 Apr 2026 23:02:10 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285185/Celtics-Rout-76ers-123-91-In-Game-1-Jayson-Tatum-Posts-25-Point-Double-Double</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Tatum_Jayson_250502.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>NBA Scheduled To Hold 2026 Draft Pick Tiebreakers On Monday</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285174/NBA-Scheduled-To-Hold-2026-Draft-Pick-Tiebreakers-On-Monday</link>
      <description><p>The NBA is set to hold tiebreakers for selections in the 2026 NBA Draft on Monday, April 20. If the tie is between lottery teams, these tiebreakers set the order pre-Draft Lottery. The actual lottery odds will be split evenly between the tied teams.</p>
<p>Non-lottery teams will also have their order decided by the tiebreakers. The second-round draft order will then be the inverse of the first-round.</p>
<p>The ties to be broken are:</p>
<p>4/5: Sacramento Kings and Utah Jazz</p>
<p>7/8: Dallas Mavericks and New Orleans Pelicans*</p>
<p>16/17/18: Orlando Magic*, Philadelphia 76ers*<em>,</em> Phoenix Suns*</p>
<p>19/20: Atlanta Hawks* and Toronto Raptors</p>
<p>22/23: Cleveland Cavaliers* and Houston Rockets*</p>
<p>24/25: Los Angeles Lakers and New York Knicks</p>
<p>*These picks will be conveyed to the following teams:</p>
<p>New Orleans Pelicans: Atlanta Hawks or Milwaukee Bucks (Atlanta receives the more favorable selection)</p>
<p>Orlando Magic: Memphis Grizzlies</p>
<p>Philadelphia 76ers: Oklahoma City Thunder</p>
<p>Phoenix Suns: Charlotte Hornets</p>
<p>Atlanta Hawks: San Antonio Spurs</p>
<p>Cleveland Cavaliers: Atlanta Hawks</p>
<p>Houston Rockets: Philadelphia 76ers</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 19 Apr 2026 09:37:18 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285174/NBA-Scheduled-To-Hold-2026-Draft-Pick-Tiebreakers-On-Monday</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_realgm2.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Caitlin Clark To Be 'Very Intentional' About Workload This Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285188/Caitlin-Clark-To-Be-Very-Intentional-About-Workload-This-Season</link>
      <description><p>Indiana Fever guard Caitlin Clark said that she was focused on staying healthy this upcoming season after playing just 13 games last year.</p>
<p>"Taking care of my body at this point in the season is probably the most important thing," Clark said. "Whether that's recovery, whether that's pre-court treatment, whatever it is. I think it's those type of things."</p>
<p>Clark dealt with a left quad strain, a left groin strain, a right groin injury, and a bone bruise in her ankle last year.</p>
<p>Clark did not play after July 15.</p>
<p>Clark added that she is now being very intentional about her activity. </p>
<p>"Very intentional about the things that I'm trying to do," Clark said. "As a young person, you just grab your basketball shoes, and you're like, oh, let's just get up on the court and lace them up and go play. And that wasn't obviously something that was going to help me through the longevity of my career."</p>
<p>Fever head coach Stephanie White added that Clark was heading in a positive direction during Team USA training camp over the offseason.</p>
<p>"She did such a great job of finding her spots of understanding time, space, rhythm," said White. "All of her timing looked really good. It's hard when you're not playing to simulate that."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 20 Apr 2026 00:58:05 EDT</pubDate>

   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Magic Shock Top-Seeded Pistons 112-101 In Game 1</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285187/Magic-Shock-Top-Seeded-Pistons-112-101-In-Game-1</link>
      <description><p>Paolo Banchero posted 23 points, nine rebounds, and four assists Sunday night to lead eighth-seeded Orlando Magic past top-seeded Detroit 112-101 in Game 1, extending the longest home playoff losing streak in NBA history. Detroit has now dropped 11 consecutive postseason games at home, a drought stretching back to 2008.</p>
<p>Orlando never trailed, jumping out to an 18-5 lead midway through the first quarter. It marked the first time since seeding began in 1984 that a No. 1 seed failed to lead at any point in its playoff opener, per ESPN Research.</p>
<p>Cade Cunningham posted a playoff career-high 39 points for Detroit. Tobias Harris added 17, but the rest of the Pistons were largely quiet offensively. Detroit's 11-point defeat ranks tied for the third-largest margin in a playoff opener by a 60-win team.</p>
<p>Franz Wagner scored 11 of his 19 points in the fourth quarter to help close out the win. Desmond Bane and Wendell Carter Jr. each contributed 17 points for Orlando, with Jalen Suggs adding 16.</p>
<p>Detroit pulled even at 65-65 in the third quarter on a Cunningham three-pointer before Banchero answered with a pull-up jumper and Bane hit a three to restore a comfortable Orlando cushion. The Magic led 81-74 entering the fourth.</p>
<p>"It's a step in the right direction for the team, for this group, with our past track record on the road in the playoffs," Banchero said. "We've just got to get ready for Wednesday, see what we did well (and) what we can clean up. I expect (the Pistons) to come out with a lot of energy and we've just got to be ready for it."</p>
<p>Neither franchise has advanced past the first round in at least 15 years.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Jalen Green Scores 36 As Suns Eliminate Warriors, Claim No. 8 Seed</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285170/Jalen-Green-Scores-36-As-Suns-Eliminate-Warriors-Claim-No-8-Seed</link>
      <description><p>Jalen Green delivered 36 points on 14-of-20 shooting as the Phoenix Suns eliminated the Golden State Warriors 111-96 Friday, claiming the Western Conference's No. 8 seed and a first-round playoff matchup against defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder beginning Sunday.</p>
<p>Green, who averaged just 13.3 points on 37% shooting against Golden State in last year's playoffs with the Houston Rockets, shot 8-of-14 from 3-point range in a pointed performance against the same opponent.</p>
<p>"I remember what they did last year," Green said. "There's a lesson in every situation. It's never failure. You always get back up. So I for sure brought some of that energy from last year into tonight's game, and we capitalized."</p>
<p>Devin Booker added 20 points and Jordan Goodwin had 19 points, nine rebounds and six steals. Stephen Curry, held to 17 points on 4-of-16 shooting, could not generate clean looks against a stifling Phoenix defense. Brandin Podziemski led Golden State with 23 points.</p>
<p>Booker and Draymond Green were both assessed technical fouls and ejected late in the fourth quarter following a prolonged verbal exchange.</p>
<p>Phoenix led by five at halftime before building a 69-53 advantage midway through the third quarter. Golden State briefly threatened when Curry's 3-pointer cut the margin to 85-78 with 9:30 remaining, but the Suns responded with seven consecutive points to put the game away.</p>
<p>The result carried added weight given Phoenix's play-in history. The Suns blew an 11-point fourth-quarter lead in a Tuesday loss to Portland before bouncing back to avoid becoming the first team to lose both home play-in games since the format was established in 2021.</p>
<p>"Sometimes the road's not always the one that you choose," Suns coach Jordan Ott said. "Sometimes it's the least traveled. So we found our way in. Now it's on to the next thing."</p>
<p>Kristaps Porzingis played just 15 minutes for Golden State, managing 11 points while working through right ankle soreness sustained Wednesday against the Clippers.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 18 Apr 2026 16:54:44 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285170/Jalen-Green-Scores-36-As-Suns-Eliminate-Warriors-Claim-No-8-Seed</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Green_Jalen_phx_251112.jpg" />
   </item>

      <description><p>The Milwaukee Bucks are looking for a new head coach after parting ways with Doc Rivers after the season. For the first time since 2018, GM Jon Horst is leading a real search, since the last two hires were guarantees for the Bucks. Adrian Griffin was widely reported to be the preferred candidate of Giannis Antetokounmpo, and Rivers was the only one considered when the team fired Griffin.</p>
<p>Former head coaches and Bucks assistants Taylor Jenkins and Darvin Ham have been linked to the opening, but Horst is expected to cast a wide net. Horst is facing a challenge of selling the job to potential hires without knowing what his roster could look like next season.</p>
<p>Jenkins has also been linked to potential openings in Washington and Orlando. The Wizards recently said that they expect to retain Brian Keefe. The Magic may not see resolution to Jamahl Mosley's status until the team's playoff run ends. Orlando beat the Charlotte Hornets on Friday to advance to the Eastern Conference Playoffs at the #8 seed against the Detroit Pistons.</p></description>

      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285167/Trail-Blazers-Not-Looking-To-Spend-Big-Money-On-A-Head-Coach</link>
      <description><p>The Portland Trail Blazers may move on from Tiago Splitter, despite the interim head coach leading the team to their first playoff appearance in five years. If they Blazers do go for a new coach, they aren't looking to spend big money to bring one to Portland.</p>
<p>New Trail Blazers owner Tom Dundon apparently doesn't want to spend more than $1.5 million per season on a head coach. That's far less than what most head coaches make, and is in line with what teams pay top assistant coaches. That figure is also far less than what top college coaches makes. That could impact Portland's ability to hire a quality leader on the sidelines.</p>
<p>Splitter is expected to be a candidate to keep the job, if a new deal can be worked out. After taking over one game into the season, Splitter led the Blazers to a 42-39 record, then a win in the Play-In Tournament to claim the seventh seed in the Western Conference Playoffs.</p></description>

      <title>Steve Kerr Unsure If He'll Return As Warriors Head Coach Next Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285166/Steve-Kerr-Unsure-If-Hell-Return-As-Warriors-Head-Coach-Next-Season</link>
      <description><p>Steve Kerr acknowledged the possibility of not returning as head coach of the Golden State Warriors on Friday following their loss at the Phoenix Suns in the play-in. Kerr is without a contract for the 2026-27 season, which was a purposeful decision as he evaluates his future.</p>
<p>"I don't know what's going to happen," Kerr said. "I still love coaching, but I get it. These jobs all have an expiration date. There is a run that happens, and when the run ends, sometimes it's time for new blood and new ideas."</p>
<p>Kerr will take a few weeks to contemplate whether he wants to keep coaching before meeting with Mike Dunleavy and Joe Lacob.</p>
<p>"If [my time is done], then I will be nothing but grateful for the most amazing opportunity any person could have to coach this franchise in front of our fans and to coach Steph Curry, [Draymond Green], the whole group," Kerr said. "It may still go on. It may not. I don't know at this point. But we all need to step away a little bit and then reconvene."</p>
<p>Kerr has won four NBA championships in his 12 seasons as head coach of the Warriors. Erik Spoelstra is the only coach with a longer tenure with his current team.</p>
<p>Since winning the 2022 NBA championship, <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Golden-State-Warriors/9/Playoff-History">the Warriors have advanced to the second round of the playoffs twice and also missed the playoffs altogether in 2024 and 2026</a>.</p></description>

      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Kerr_Steve_gsw_240113.jpg" />

      <title>Stephen Curry Interested In Signing Extension With Warriors</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285165/Stephen-Curry-Interested-In-Signing-Extension-With-Warriors</link>
      <description><p>Stephen Curry has one season remaining on his contract at $62.6 million for 2026-27 with the Golden State Warriors and he expressed interest in signing an extension. Curry, who most recently signed the one-year extension in 2024, has yet to hold talks with the Warriors.</p>
<p>Curry was also asked if he can envision himself playing more than just the one season he has left on his contract.</p>
<p>"Multiple, for sure," said Curry.</p>
<p>While Curry has continued to play at a high level, he dealt with knee issues this seasons and the supporting cast around him is clearly below the true elite title contenders. But the Warriors were aggressive at the trade deadline as they held negotiations on deals for Giannis Antetokounmpo, Kawhi Leonard and Jaren Jackson Jr. The Warriors are expected to pursue a significant upgrade again in the offseason and have also been linked with LeBron James.</p>
<p>The Warriors are also navigating the future of Steve Kerr, who is not under contract for next season.</p>
<p>"I want Coach to be happy," Curry said. "I want him to be excited for the job. I want him to believe he's the right guy for the job. I want him to have an opportunity to enjoy what he does, whatever that means for him. Everybody's plan is their own. He knows how I feel about him."</p></description>

      <description><p>Aliyah Boston has agreed to a four-year, $6.3 million contract extension with the Indiana Fever. The contract is the largest contract in terms of total salary in WNBA history.</p>

      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285160/Ty-Lue-To-Remain-Head-Coach-Of-Clippers-For-A-Long-Long-Time</link>
      <description><p>Los Angeles Clippers president of basketball operations Lawrence Frank stated Friday that the franchise never considered replacing Tyronn Lue as head coach, even during their 6-21 start to the 2025-26 season.</p>
<p>"Even at our lowest point, when we were 6-21, there never ever was any consideration of Ty not being the coach," Frank said at his end-of-season news conference. "Ty's going to be the coach here for a long, long time."</p>
<p>Lue guided the Clippers to a 42-40 final record before a fourth-quarter collapse against the Golden State Warriors ended their season in Wednesday's play-in tournament. Lue remains under contract through 2029 and is believed to be tied with Erik Spoelstra as the NBA's second highest paid head coach behind Steve Kerr.</p>
<p>Frank credited Lue's steadiness through a year that included significant midseason roster upheaval.</p>
<p>"I thought Ty and the staff did an unbelievable job of, every day, showing up with a great spirit, and just kept at it," Frank said. "Ty's an incredible coach, an incredible partner. And one of the characteristics we look for, not just in staff, but also in players, are people who are driven to improve."</p>
<p>Lue led the Cleveland Cavaliers to the 2016 NBA championship, overcoming a 3-1 Finals deficit against the 73-win Warriors, before joining the Clippers as an assistant in 2019 and being elevated to head coach in 2020 to replace Doc Rivers. He guided Los Angeles to the franchise's only conference finals appearance in 2021.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Los-Angeles-Clippers/12/playoff-history">The Clippers have not won a playoff series in five consecutive seasons</a>.</p></description>

      <title>Oscar Schmidt Passes Away At Age 68</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285159/Oscar-Schmidt-Passes-Away-At-Age-68</link>
      <description><p>Basketball Hall of Famer Oscar Schmidt has passed away at the age of 68 due to a long battle with a brain tumor. Schmidt played in a record-tying five Olympics and four World Cups for Brazil and became the all-time leading scorer in both tournaments.</p>
<p>"The biggest player of Brazilian basketball history bids farewell as an absolute symbol of sport, the holder of a trajectory that redefined the boundaries of what was possible in a court," the Brazil Basketball Confederation said in a statement. "His death closes an era. But his greatness remains."</p>
<p>Schmidt retired in 2003 at 45. He passed Kareem Abdul-Jabbar to be the unofficial career highest scorer and his known tally of 49,737 points for club and country was eclipsed by LeBron James in 2024.</p>
<p>Schmidt was inducted into the FIBA Hall of Fame in 2010 and the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame in 2013</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 17 Apr 2026 21:57:23 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285159/Oscar-Schmidt-Passes-Away-At-Age-68</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_realgm.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Anthony Edwards Questionable For Game 1 With 'Right Knee Injury Maintenance'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285158/Anthony-Edwards-Questionable-For-Game-1-With-Right-Knee-Injury-Maintenance</link>
      <description><p>Anthony Edwards has bene listed as questionable by the Minnesota Timberwolves for Game 1 of their first round playoff series agains the Denver Nuggets due to "right knee injury maintenance." Edwards is expected to play in Saturday's game and was a "full go" when the Wolves scrimmaged this week.</p>
<p>Edwards missed 11 of the final 14 games of the regular season due to the knee condition. Edwards fell short of the NBA's 65-game minimum for regular season awards.</p>
<p>Edwards most recently played last Friday and looked healthy.</p>
<p>Minnesota last met the Nuggets during the 2024 playoffs when they were victorious in Game 7 of their second round series. The Nuggets rested most of their rotation in their final regular season game against the San Antonio Spurs as they signaled an apparent preference to avoid the Wolves to instead play the Houston Rockets in the first round. But the Nuggets upset the Spurs in the game to setup a matchup against Edwards and the Wolves.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 17 Apr 2026 21:52:45 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285158/Anthony-Edwards-Questionable-For-Game-1-With-Right-Knee-Injury-Maintenance</guid>

   <item>
      <title>Clippers Signal They Want To Keep Kawhi Leonard</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285157/Clippers-Signal-They-Want-To-Keep-Kawhi-Leonard</link>
      <description><p>Los Angeles Clippers president of basketball operations Lawrence Frank publicly committed to building around Kawhi Leonard on Friday, days after <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285131/Kawhi-Leonard-Defers-On-Clippers-Future-As-Contract-Extension-Window-Approaches">the 34-year-old forward deflected questions about his own future</a> following the team's play-in elimination.</p>
<p>"Our plan is to win with Kawhi," Frank said at his end-of-season news conference. "At the appropriate time, we'll sit down with Kawhi, and very similar to 2024, lay out our plan. And if our goals are aligned, then we'd like to win with Kawhi."</p>
<p>Leonard, who turns 35 in June, posted career-best scoring numbers this season at 27.9 points per game across 65 regular-season appearances, his second season with at least 60 games played since 2018-19. Despite the individual production, the Clippers missed the playoffs for the first time in a healthy Leonard season.</p>
<p>Following Wednesday's play-in loss to the Golden State Warriors, Leonard offered little insight into his intentions.</p>
<p>"Let me cry about this loss a little bit more," he said. "We'll have our discussions when that time comes."</p>
<p>The Clippers executed significant roster moves at the trade deadline, sending James Harden to the Cleveland Cavaliers for Darius Garland and trading Ivica Zubac to the Indiana Pacers for a package that included Bennedict Mathurin, which were moves that raised questions about the franchise's direction. The Warriors also contacted the Clippers on a potential Leonard trade ahead of the deadline. </p>
<p>Frank characterized the organization as competitive rather than rebuilding, pointing to 15 consecutive winning seasons, emerging young talent, draft capital and projected cap flexibility as evidence the Clippers remain positioned to contend.</p>
<p>"As we build that bridge from competitive to contender, we've put ourselves in a very good position with emerging young players, draft capital and cap space going forward," Frank said.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285148/Darius-Garland-Ty-Lue-Only-Members-Of-Clippers-Certain-To-Return-Next-Season">The Athletic reported this week that Garland and head coach Ty Lue are the only ones certain to return next season</a>.</p>
<p>An ongoing NBA investigation into Leonard's endorsement deal with now-bankrupt sponsor Aspiration also hung over the season. Frank maintained Friday that the probe had no bearing on operations and expressed full confidence in the organization's position.</p>
<p>"If you know Steve [Ballmer] and know Steve's integrity, you know there's nothing to it," Frank said. "We believe and we're very confident we're on the right side of this."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 17 Apr 2026 21:47:06 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285157/Clippers-Signal-They-Want-To-Keep-Kawhi-Leonard</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Leonard_Kawhi_lac_250107.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Kevin Durant Questionable For Game 1 With Knee Contusion</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285156/Kevin-Durant-Questionable-For-Game-1-With-Knee-Contusion</link>
      <description><p>Kevin Durant sustained a right knee contusion during practice this week and has been added to the Houston Rockets' injury report ahead of Game 1 on Saturday at the Los Angeles Lakers. The Rockets are optimistic that Durant's knee injury will not be a significant issue during the series and is listed as questionable for Game 1. </p>
<p>The Rockets face an injury-depleted Lakers team that are without both Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves indefinitely. Houston enters their first round series as significant favorites against the Lakers. Durant will battle against LeBron James in the postseason for the first time since the 2018 Finals.</p>
<p>During his first season with the Rockets following his trade from the Phoenix Suns, Durant averaged 26.0 points, 5.5 rebounds and 4.8 assists. Durant took on a significant role in the Rockets' offense as the team lost Fred VanVleet before the start of the season with a torn ACL.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 17 Apr 2026 21:41:37 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285156/Kevin-Durant-Questionable-For-Game-1-With-Knee-Contusion</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Durant_Kevin_hou_251010.jpg" />
   </item>
